obligations.obligation:
Regulation 9(4) of the
Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“(4) The Trading Licensee shall make an appropriate application accompanied by
prescribed fees for up-gradation of its licence to a higher category or down-gradation
Order in Petition No. 14/TD/2026 Page 2of its licence to a ...
obligations.short_summary: Application for licence category change required to meet regulatory conditions.

obligations.obligation:
Regulation 15(1) of the Trading Licence Regulations 2020 provides as under:
“15(1) Upgradation of licence:
(a) The Trading Licensee may at any time during a particular year apply for a
higher category licence on the basis of the volume proposed to be traded in a particular
Year.
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
(b) The Trading Licensee shall file an application before the Commission for
upgradation of its licence to a higher category as soon as he expects the trading
volume to exceed the trading volume limit specified for the Trading Licensee’s existing
category.
obligations.short_summary: Application for license upgrade required upon exceeding trading volume limits.

obligations.obligation:
(c) The Trading Licensee shall be granted an opportunity of being heard by the
Commission before taking a decision on the application
(d) The Trading Licensee shall be required to submit the audited special balance sheet
to support the compliance of Net Worth requirement for higher category licence.
obligations.short_summary: Opportunity to be heard before decision; Submission of audited balance sheet required.

obligations.obligation:
(e) The Trading Licensee shall pay licence fee applicable to the higher category licence
if in a particular Year the licence has been upgraded:
Provided that in case a Trading Licensee has already paid licence fee applicable for
its existing lower category licence in a particular year, then the Trad...
obligations.short_summary: Pay higher category licence fee if upgraded, otherwise pay difference.

obligations.obligation:
As regards the
net worth requirements for Category ‘IV”, Regulation 3 (3) (a) of the Trading Licence
Regulations, 2020 provides that a person applying for a Category ‘IV’ trading licence
should have a net worth of Rs. 10 crore and should have maintained minimum current
ratio and liquidity ratio of 1...
obligations.short_summary: Basic rule-based detection (LLM skipped for speed)

obligations.obligation:
The
licensee shall remain bound by the terms and conditions contained in the order dated
23.10.2023 in Petition No. 257/TD/2023, whereby it was issued with Category ‘V’
trading licence under Trading Licence Regulations, 2020.
obligations.short_summary: Must adhere to Category ‘V’ trading license terms and conditions.

obligations.obligation:
Let an extract copy of this order be sent to the Central Government (Ministry of
Power) and the Central Electricity Authority in terms of sub-section (7) of Section 15
of the Electricity Act, 2003, for their information and record.
